mnemonics.title: Types of Requirements
mnemonics.content: FUN PRSG: Functional, Non-functional, Performance, Reliability, Scalability, Growth (types to remember)

mnemonics.title: Capacity Planning Goals
mnemonics.content: SMART CAP: Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Timely, Cost-effective, Aligned, Prioritized (for goal setting)

mnemonics.title: Non-Functional Requirements
mnemonics.content: SPARSEC: Security, Performance, Availability, Reliability, Scalability, Efficiency, Capacity (remember non-functional types)

acronyms.usage: Contract defining expected service performance metrics and availability targets
Average time between system failures - measures reliability
Average time to restore system after failure - measures maintainability
Throughput metric for measuring system processing capacity
Overall performance level of a service including reliability and responsiveness

visualTips.visualization: Think of availability as 'nines': 99% = 2 nines = 87.6 hours downtime/year, 99.9% = 3 nines = 8.76 hours, 99.99% = 4 nines = 52.6 minutes
Vertical = growing UP (taller tower), Horizontal = growing WIDE (more towers side by side)
Think of a mountain: average is the base height, peak is the mountain summit - capacity must handle both

analogies.analogy: Like planning the number of checkout counters in a supermarket - too few causes long lines, too many wastes money. Need to match demand patterns.
Like adding lanes to a highway. Vertical = making existing lanes wider (more cars per lane), Horizontal = adding more lanes.
Reliability is how often a car breaks down (fewer breakdowns = more reliable). Availability is if you can use the car when you need it (car in repair shop = unavailable).
Like minimum passing marks in an exam - must meet this standard, but you can exceed it for better performance.
